job_description:
Credentialing Specialist

Roles & Responsibilities

  • Process and manage Physician, Non-Physician, and Laboratory credentialing, re-credentialing, and enrollment applications with federal, state, and commercial insurance payers.
  • Prepare, review, and submit credentialing applications to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with payer requirements.

  • Maintain and update provider and laboratory records, including CLIA, licenses, DEA, CAQH profiles, certifications, malpractice insurance, and other required documents.
  • Track credentialing deadlines, license renewals, CLIA renewals, re-credentialing cycles, and contract expiration dates to ensure continuous compliance.
  • Communicate with insurance payers, providers, clients, and internal stakeholders to resolve credentialing issues and obtain required documentation.
  • Ensure compliance with federal regulations, state regulations, payer guidelines, and organizational policies.
  • Maintain accurate credentialing data and documentation within credentialing systems, databases, and tracking tools.
  • Generate and maintain credentialing status reports, productivity reports, and aging reports for management review.
  • Identify and escalate credentialing delays, discrepancies, or compliance concerns to leadership in a timely manner.
  • Participate in client meetings and provide credentialing status updates as required.
  • Collaborate with team members to achieve departmental productivity, quality, and turnaround time goals.
  • Support process improvement initiatives to enhance credentialing efficiency and accuracy.
  • Perform additional credentialing and administrative duties as assigned.

Requirements

  • 2+ years of experience with medical credentialing.
  • Strong knowledge of provider enrollment and credentialing processes.
  • Experience with CAQH, PECOS, NPPES, and commercial payer portals.
  • Strong communication, time management, analytical, and logical thinking skills.
  • Proactive approach and interest in acquiring new knowledge.
  • Bachelor's degree required.
